items.question: Do you accept Medicaid?
items.answer: Yes. We accept Medicaid and most major insurance plans. We also offer options for uninsured individuals and same-week intake. Call (609) 242-0086 to confirm your specific plan and to schedule an intake.

items.question: How long is the IOP program?
items.answer: The Intensive Outpatient Program runs three evenings per week with a weekly 30-minute individual session. Length depends on clinical need; most participants complete the program in 8–12 weeks. We will work with you on the timeline that fits your recovery.

items.question: Do you offer evening hours?
items.answer: Yes. We are open Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 9:00 PM, and our IOP groups typically meet in the evening so people can attend around work or family schedules. Weekend sessions are by appointment.

items.question: Do you offer services in Spanish?
items.answer: Yes — we offer bilingual services. Call us at (609) 242-0086 and let us know you would prefer Spanish; we will match you with a Spanish-speaking clinician.

items.question: Is the first session free?
items.answer: Your first contact is a free, confidential phone call with our intake team — no commitment, no charge. If you decide to move forward, we schedule a clinical assessment at our standard rate. Many insurance plans cover the assessment. Call (609) 242-0086 to start.

items.question: Do you see adolescents?
items.answer: Our primary programs serve adults. If you are looking for adolescent substance use services, call us at (609) 242-0086 and we will refer you to an appropriate partner program in Ocean County.

items.question: Do you provide transportation?
items.answer: We do not provide transportation directly, but our office is on U.S. Route 9 in Lanoka Harbor with easy access from Ocean County. Call (609) 242-0086 if transportation is a barrier — we will help you think through options including public transit, family support, and community resources.

items.question: Is your anger management program state-approved?
items.answer: Yes. Our anger management program is state-approved and includes trigger identification, healthy communication, and optional reiki and mindfulness components. Call (609) 242-0086 for current group availability and court / referral paperwork.

items.question: Do you work with NJ DOT referrals for impaired driving?
items.answer: Yes — we are an NJ DOT–approved provider for impaired-driver counseling. Call (609) 242-0086 with your referral paperwork in hand; we will explain the steps, schedule your intake, and confirm the program length required by your case.

items.question: Is there a wait list?
items.answer: No. We offer same-week intake for most programs. Call (609) 242-0086 today — if we cannot see you this week, we will tell you on the phone and help you find a faster path to care.
